title: Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice

seo.title: How to Make Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice — Easy Korean Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice. This easy Korean recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

easy Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice recipe
authentic Korean Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Korean Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice — a beloved Korean classic

description: Patbingsu is a refreshing and iconic Korean shaved ice dessert, perfect for cooling down on a hot day. This delightful treat features finely shaved ice, often made from frozen milk, topped generously with sweet red bean paste and a variety of colorful toppings. It's a harmonious blend of textures and flavors that captivates the palate.

ingredients.notes: or 3 cups milk + 1 cup sweetened condensed milk

instructions.title: Prepare the Red Beans
instructions.text: Rinse the pre-soaked red beans thoroughly. In a large pot, cover the beans with fresh water and bring to a boil. Boil for 10 minutes, then drain the water. This step helps remove bitterness. Cover the beans with fresh water again (about 4-5 cups) and bring to a boil.

instructions.title: Cook the Red Beans
instructions.text: Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer the red beans for 30-45 minutes, or until the beans are very tender and easily mashable. Check occasionally and add more hot water if needed to keep them submerged. The beans should be soft but still hold their shape.

instructions.title: Make the Sweet Red Bean Paste (Pat)
instructions.text: Drain most of the cooking liquid from the beans, leaving about 1/4 cup. Add sugar and salt to the pot. Using a potato masher or the back of a spoon, mash the beans to your desired consistency. For a smoother paste, you can blend a portion of the beans. Cook on low heat for another 5-10 minutes, stirring constantly, until the paste thickens. Let it cool completely.

instructions.title: Prepare the Milk Ice Base
instructions.text: If using, mix 3 cups of whole milk with 1 cup of sweetened condensed milk. Pour the milk mixture into a shallow freezer-safe pan or ice cube trays. Freeze overnight or for at least 8 hours until completely solid. This creates a creamy, flavorful ice base.

instructions.title: Shave the Milk Ice
instructions.text: Once the milk is frozen solid, remove it from the freezer. Break the frozen milk into smaller chunks if necessary. Use a bingsu machine to shave the ice into fine, fluffy flakes. If you don't have a bingsu machine, you can use a food processor to pulse the ice until it reaches a finely crushed consistency, though it won't be as fine as traditional bingsu.

instructions.title: Assemble Patbingsu
instructions.text: Divide the shaved milk ice into serving bowls, mounding it high. Generously top each bowl with the prepared sweet red bean paste. Arrange the injeolmi rice cake cubes and fresh fruit around the red bean paste. Drizzle with additional sweetened condensed milk if desired, and add a scoop of vanilla ice cream or mochi for extra indulgence. Serve immediately and enjoy!

tips: For the smoothest and fluffiest shaved ice, freeze the milk in a shallow pan and use a dedicated bingsu machine. A food processor can work in a pinch for a coarser texture.
Making your own red bean paste allows you to control the sweetness. Adjust the sugar content to your preference. You can also add a pinch of cinnamon or vanilla for a subtle flavor twist.
Patbingsu is highly customizable! Experiment with various toppings like corn flakes for crunch, various syrups, different types of mochi, or even green tea powder for a unique twist.
article: Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice is a beloved Korean Desserts dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
